patterns.name: Duplicated ConnectOptions designated-initializer in pybind client constructors
patterns.rationale: The py::init lambda for each pybind-exposed client (KVClient, ObjectClient, HeteroClient) builds a ConnectOptions struct by copying the same constructor arguments (host, port, connectTimeoutMs, requestTimeoutMs, token, clientPublicKey, clientPrivateKey, serverPublicKey, accessKey, secretKey, tenantId, enableCrossNodeConnection, enableExclusiveConnection) into identically-named designated initializers. The blocks are near-identical (13+ shared fields) and differ only by a trailing client-specific field or a hardcoded value. A shared helper that maps the common connection arguments to a ConnectOptions would remove ~15 duplicated lines per client and keep the field list in one place, avoiding drift when ConnectOptions gains a field.

locations.existing_code:
ConnectOptions connectOpts{ .host = host,
                                        .port = port,
                                        .connectTimeoutMs = connectTimeoutMs,
                                        .requestTimeoutMs = reqTimeoutMs,

                                        .token = token,
                                        .clientPublicKey = clientPublicKey,
                                        .clientPrivateKey = clientPrivateKey,
                                        .serverPublicKey = serverPublicKey,
                                        .accessKey = accessKey,
                                        .secretKey = secretKey,
                                        .tenantId = tenantId,
                                        .enableCrossNodeConnection = enableCrossNodeConnection,
                                        .enableExclusiveConnection = enableExclusiveConnection,
                                        .fastTransportMemSize = fastTransportMemSize };

ConnectOptions connectOpts{
                .host = host,
                .port = port,
                .connectTimeoutMs = connectTimeoutMs,
                .requestTimeoutMs = reqTimeoutMs,
                .token = token,
                .clientPublicKey = clientPublicKey,
                .clientPrivateKey = clientPrivateKey,
                .serverPublicKey = serverPublicKey,
                .accessKey = accessKey,
                .secretKey = secretKey,
                .tenantId = tenantId,
                .enableCrossNodeConnection = false,
                .enableExclusiveConnection = enableExclusiveConnection,
            };

ConnectOptions connectOpts{
                .host = host,
                .port = port,
                .connectTimeoutMs = connectTimeoutMs,
                .requestTimeoutMs = reqTimeoutMs,
                .token = token,
                .clientPublicKey = clientPublicKey,
                .clientPrivateKey = clientPrivateKey,
                .serverPublicKey = serverPublicKey,
                .accessKey = accessKey,
                .secretKey = secretKey,
                .tenantId = tenantId,
                .enableCrossNodeConnection = enableCrossNodeConnection,
                .enableExclusiveConnection = enableExclusiveConnection,
                .enableRemoteH2D = enableRemoteH2D
            };

patterns.name: Duplicated MSet buffer-collection loop in KVClient MSet bindings
patterns.rationale: Two MSet-style pybind bindings in pybind_register_kv.cpp contain a byte-identical block that builds an MSetParam and iterates the input buffers to accumulate totalSize and collect StringViews. The loop body (py::buffer_info construction, size accumulation, StringView wrap, emplace_back) is copy-pasted verbatim; extracting it into a small helper would remove the repeated buffer-unpacking logic and keep a single place to update if the buffer handling changes.
